steps.label: cause
steps.text: Malolactic fermentation (MLF) is induced, in which lactic acid bacteria convert malic acid to lactic acid

steps.label: mechanism
steps.text: Malic acid (sharp, tart, green-apple flavour) is converted to lactic acid (softer, milky, creamier flavour); CO2 is released; the total acidity of the wine decreases and pH rises

steps.label: effect
steps.text: The wine shows reduced, softer acidity, a rounder texture on the palate, and may develop buttery or creamy flavour compounds (diacetyl) if bacterial activity is high

steps.label: exam_formulation
steps.text: MLF converts sharp malic acid to softer lactic acid, reducing acidity and adding a creamy texture to the wine.

sat_relevance: Use when assessing acidity softness or creamy texture in white wines. MLF is a deliberate winemaking choice — its presence or absence directly affects the wine's style and palate texture.
